title: new_challenge: broken clock silently produces ts=0; nonce fails freshness check
description: core/src/auth.rs:24-33: new_challenge() calls SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap_or_default(). If the system clock is before the Unix epoch, unwrap_or_default() returns Duration::ZERO, so the nonce timestamp bytes are [0,0,0,0]. The relay then calls nonce_is_fresh(&nonce) (ws.rs:363), which computes age = current_unix_time - 0 = current_unix_time (e.g., 1.7 billion seconds), which is >> CHALLENGE_TTL_SECS (300). The freshness check rejects the nonce with AUTH_FAILED. The client receives an opaque auth failure with no diagnostic information — the relay rejected its own nonce. Contrast with transport.rs:447-451 where the client side uses context() to fail explicitly. Fix: use context() or an explicit check in new_challenge() to propagate a clear error when the clock is broken, matching the defensive posture in transport.rs.
